title: Do not hard-code secrets
description: Hard-coded secrets such as passwords, API keys, or cryptographic keys in source code can be easily discovered and exploited by attackers. Storing secrets in code exposes them to version control, code review, and anyone with access to the source, increasing the risk of unauthorized access and data breaches.
recommendation: Never store secrets directly in source code. Use secure secret management solutions such as environment variables, dedicated secret stores (e.g., AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault), or configuration files excluded from version control.
impact: Exposure of hard-coded secrets can lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, and compromise of systems or user data.

examples.good: String password = System.getenv("DB_PASSWORD"); // Compliant: secret loaded from environment variable

checks.message: Never store secrets directly in source code. Use secure secret management solutions such as environment variables, dedicated secret stores (e.g., AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault), or configuration files excluded from version control.
